_comment: Example Claude Code settings.json for the ai-pipeline-scaffold. Copy to settings.json and trim to the actual tool surface your pipeline needs. The permissions.allow list is the load-bearing safety rail referenced throughout the agents and skills; scoping it tightly is how you avoid shipping --dangerously-skip-permissions in headless sessions. JSON does not support inline comments, so annotations live in this _comment field and in CUSTOMIZE.md.
_notes_for_adopters: Start from this file, rename to settings.json, then trim the allow list to only what your pipeline actually invokes. A smaller surface = safer failure mode (loud denial rather than silent bypass).
Never add '--dangerously-skip-permissions' to any agent spawn or subprocess call — the scaffold's publisher and implementer both refuse this explicitly.
If your project uses paired forks, settings.json lives in the private fork only; the public fork inherits none of this config when forkers clone it.
The 'deny' list is belt-and-suspenders — the allow list is the primary gate. A command absent from 'allow' is already denied.

settings.json itself should be gitignored if it carries user-specific permissions; settings.local.json is the conventional name for purely-local overrides (already in templates/public-scaffold/.gitignore).
